sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 0.1g. saturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for a tuber, this mostly lives or dies on carb behaviour.
sections.subtitleText: fat is 0.1g. saturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for a tuber, this mostly lives or dies on carb behaviour.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 17.5g. glycemic index, fibre, and sugar are doing most of the work. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.
sections.subtitleText: carbs is 17.5g. glycemic index, fibre, and sugar are doing most of the work. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: protein is 2g. protein quality is clearly one of the selling points. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.
sections.subtitleText: protein is 2g. protein quality is clearly one of the selling points. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin c at 22% DV, vitamin a at 0% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin c at 22% DV, vitamin a at 0% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: potassium at 9% DV, magnesium at 6% DV, iron at 4% DV stand out most. that stays fairly limited overall.
sections.subtitleText: potassium at 9% DV, magnesium at 6% DV, iron at 4% DV stand out most. that stays fairly limited overall.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first. very practical staple food because cheap, common, and easy to build meals around, which matters for a tuber category built on energy foods. can be fairly filling because especially when eaten in less processed forms. brings some useful potassium because not amazing, but enough to matter a bit in the category.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides. glycemic behaviour is only middling because not as clean as the best tubers on the carb-behaviour axis. fibre is only modest because decent, but not category-leading. micronutrient return is fine rather than elite because strong enough to be respectable, not enough to be exceptional.

closing.summary: White Potato stands out for very practical staple food and can be fairly filling. but it also gets held back by glycemic behaviour is only middling and fibre is only modest. Best when you want practical carbs and the rest of the profile is still reasonably clean.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want practical carbs and the rest of the profile is still reasonably clean.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
